title: varronia longipedunculata
intro: Varronia longipedunculata, also known as the African teak or bush tea tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Boraginaceae. The medicinal properties of Varronia longipedunculata include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of malaria, and support digestive health. Varronia longipedunculata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are commonly consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
